+#### Motion & Animation
 
 **Prefer CSS-only for simple cases.** Only reach for framer-motion when you need gesture handling, layout animations, or complex orchestration.
 
-| Technique | CSS-only (Tailwind) | framer-motion |
+| Technique | Vanilla Tailwind/CSS | framer-motion |
 |-----------|---------------------|---------------|
-| Entrance fade-up | `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4` | `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 16 }} animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` |
+| Entrance fade-up | Custom keyframe (see below) | `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 16 }} animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` |
 | Hover scale | `hover:scale-[1.02] transition-transform` | `whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }}` |
-| Skeleton loader | `animate-pulse bg-muted rounded` | not needed |
-| Page transition | View Transitions API | `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` |
+| Skeleton loader | `animate-pulse bg-slate-200 dark:bg-slate-700 rounded` | not needed |
+| Page transition | View Transitions API (Chrome; use progressive enhancement) | `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` |
 | Scroll reveal | `IntersectionObserver` + CSS class toggle | `whileInView` |
+
+**Custom keyframe for fade-up (add to Tailwind config or CSS):**
+
+Tailwind v3 — `tailwind.config.js`:
+```js
+theme: { extend: {
+  keyframes: { fadeSlideUp: { '0%': { opacity: '0', transform: 'translateY(16px)' }, '100%': { opacity: '1', transform: 'translateY(0)' } } },
+  animation: { 'fade-slide-up': 'fadeSlideUp 0.3s ease-out forwards' }
+} }
+```
+
+Tailwind v4 — main CSS file:
+```css
+@theme { --animate-fade-slide-up: fadeSlideUp 0.3s ease-out forwards; }
+@keyframes fadeSlideUp { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(16px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }
+```
+
+If `tailwindcss-animate` plugin is detected in the project, use its classes (`animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4`) instead.
 
 **Performance rules:**
 - Only animate `transform` and `opacity` (GPU-composited properties)
-- Avoid `will-change` unless profiling shows jank — it causes memory overhead; if used, add via JS before animation and remove after
+- Avoid `will-change` unless profiling shows jank — add via JS before animation, remove after
 - Use CSS `contain: layout paint` for animated sections to limit repaint scope
-- Use `content-visibility: auto` for below-fold sections to reduce initial render cost
 - Durations: micro-interactions 150ms, reveals 250–300ms, page transitions 350–400ms. Nothing over 500ms.
 - `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` → disable all motion except opacity fades
 
 #### Responsive Strategy
 
-- **Marketing/public pages**: mobile-first (`sm:` → `md:` → `lg:` → `xl:`)
-- **Admin dashboards/data-dense interfaces**: desktop-first, then scale down with responsive overrides
-- **Component-level**: use `@container` queries (Tailwind `@container` variant) for cards, sidebar widgets, and dashboard panels — superior to viewport queries when components render in varying parent widths
+- **All Tailwind implementations are mobile-first** — unprefixed utilities are the mobile base, `sm:`/`md:`/`lg:`/`xl:` add breakpoint overrides
+- For admin dashboards, **design** desktop-first (start your thinking at desktop) but **implement** mobile-first in Tailwind. Use `max-md:` variants only for specific mobile overrides where it's cleaner.
+- **Container queries**: use `@container` (requires `@tailwindcss/container-queries` plugin in v3, native in v4) for cards and dashboard panels. Parent needs `@container` class for `container-type: inline-size`.
 - Touch targets: minimum 44×44px (`min-h-11 min-w-11`)
-- Typography: fluid `clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 1.25rem)` for body, responsive prefixes for headings
-- Images: `<picture>` with `srcset`, serve WebP/AVIF with JPEG fallback, always set `aspect-ratio` or explicit `width`/`height` to prevent CLS
-- Data-dense components (pricing tables, comparison grids): switch to accordion or swipeable cards on mobile — never horizontal scroll
-- Navigation: drawer or bottom-sheet on mobile, full nav on desktop

+**Reference — Sticky Nav with transparent-on-scroll (vanilla Tailwind):**
+
+```tsx
+import { useState, useEffect } from "react";
+
+export function StickyNav() {
+  const [scrolled, setScrolled] = useState(false);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const onScroll = () => setScrolled(window.scrollY > 20);
+    window.addEventListener("scroll", onScroll, { passive: true });
+    return () => window.removeEventListener("scroll", onScroll);
+  }, []);
+
+  return (
+    <>
+      <a href="#main" className="sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:z-50 focus:bg-white focus:px-4 focus:py-2">
+        Skip to content
+      </a>
+      <header className={`fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-40 transition-all duration-300 ${scrolled ? "bg-white/90 shadow-sm backdrop-blur-md dark:bg-slate-950/90" : "bg-transparent"}`}>
+        <nav className="container mx-auto flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-4">
+          <a href="/" className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white">Brand</a>
+          <div className="hidden items-center gap-6 md:flex">
+            <a href="#features" className="text-sm text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-300 dark:hover:text-white">Features</a>
+            <a href="#pricing" className="text-sm text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-300 dark:hover:text-white">Pricing</a>
+            <button className="rounded-lg bg-slate-900 px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white hover:bg-slate-800 focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-slate-900 dark:bg-white dark:text-slate-900 dark:hover:bg-slate-100">
+              Get Started
+            </button>
+          </div>

+}

+import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
+
+export function Reveal({ children, className = "" }: { children: React.ReactNode; className?: string }) {
+  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
+  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const prefersReduced = window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches;
+    if (prefersReduced) { setVisible(true); return; }
+    const el = ref.current;
+    if (!el) return;
+    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { setVisible(true); observer.disconnect(); } }, { threshold: 0.15 });
+    observer.observe(el);
+    return () => observer.disconnect();
+  }, []);
+
+  return (
+    <div ref={ref} className={`transition-all duration-500 ${visible ? "translate-y-0 opacity-100" : "translate-y-4 opacity-0"} ${className}`}>
+      {children}
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+

 ## Design Principles (Always Apply)
 
 1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye. Minimum 2:1 size ratio between heading levels.
-2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality; cramped layouts signal amateur. When in doubt, add more space.
-3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do. Everything else is secondary or tertiary.
-4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show spatial relationships. Never animate for decoration.
-5. **Design for the primary device first** — Mobile-first for marketing and public-facing pages. Desktop-first for admin dashboards and data-dense interfaces. Never bolt on responsiveness as an afterthought.
-6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product. Novelty per-component is noise. Establish a system, then follow it.
-7. **Performance is UX** — A fast, simple design beats a slow, beautiful one. Target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms.

objections.description: MCP tool calls are specified with incorrect/assumed parameter schemas. There's no validation that the tool names (`mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources`, `mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution`, `mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt`) actually exist or that the parameter shapes (`goal`, `stack`, `needsAnimation`, etc.) match the real tool schemas. If these tools don't exist or have different signatures, the skill will fail silently or hallucinate tool calls. The skill needs a defensive check pattern: attempt the call, handle failure gracefully, and fall back to built-in knowledge. Right now Steps 3, 5, and 6 all say 'If MCP tools are available' but never specify HOW to detect availability — Claude Code will just attempt the call and error out.
objections.suggested_fix: Add an explicit preamble instruction: 'Before calling any MCP tool, check if it is available in the current session. If a tool call fails or the tool is not registered, silently fall back to built-in recommendations. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user.' Also add a note that the parameter schemas shown are illustrative and the skill should adapt to the actual tool schema if it differs. Consider making MCP integration a clearly marked optional enhancement section rather than weaving it into the core process flow.

objections.description: The skill instructs Claude to read `src/components/`, `tailwind.config.*`, and `package.json` but provides no fallback for when these don't exist. Many Vite+React projects use `app/` or `lib/` structures, monorepos with `packages/*/`, or have tailwind config in `postcss.config.js` with `@tailwindcss/postcss`. The skill also doesn't account for Tailwind v4 which uses CSS-based configuration (`@theme` directive in CSS files) rather than `tailwind.config.js`. This is a real-world gap that will cause the skill to skip context gathering entirely for modern setups.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace hardcoded paths with a search strategy: 'Look for Tailwind configuration in: `tailwind.config.{js,ts,mjs,cjs}`, `postcss.config.*`, or CSS files containing `@theme` (Tailwind v4). Look for components in: `src/components/`, `src/app/`, `app/`, `components/`, or any directory containing `.tsx` files with React component exports. Check `package.json` at project root or nearest `package.json` in a monorepo.' Add explicit Tailwind v4 awareness — CSS-based config with `@theme` blocks, `@variant dark`, and `@utility` directives.

objections.description: The z-index scale uses values z-60, z-70, z-80 which are NOT default Tailwind utilities. Tailwind ships with z-0 through z-50 by default. Using z-60/z-70/z-80 requires custom theme extension, but the skill presents them as if they work out of the box. A developer following this guidance will get no CSS output for those classes unless they extend their config.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Add explicit instruction to extend the Tailwind theme: `zIndex: { 60: '60', 70: '70', 80: '80' }` in tailwind.config, or (2) Use arbitrary values `z-[60]` `z-[70]` `z-[80]` in the recommendation, or (3) Restructure the scale to use default Tailwind values (z-10, z-20, z-30, z-40, z-50) and note that arbitrary values should be used for anything beyond z-50.

objections.description: The hero code uses semantic color classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `text-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-card`, `text-card-foreground`, `bg-accent`, `ring-border` — these are shadcn/ui CSS variable conventions, NOT default Tailwind. A vanilla Tailwind project will render none of these colors. The skill claims to 'default to raw Tailwind + React implementations that work with zero additional dependencies' but the code contradicts this by depending on a shadcn/ui-style color token system.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Provide truly vanilla Tailwind code examples using default palette (`bg-white dark:bg-gray-950`, `text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-50`, etc.), OR (2) Add a required setup section showing the CSS custom properties these classes depend on and the tailwind.config extension needed, OR (3) Present both variants — one for shadcn/ui projects (detected by `components.json`), one for vanilla Tailwind projects. Option 3 is cleanest. The code exemplars should match the 'zero additional dependencies' claim.

objections.description: The guidance says 'desktop-first for admin dashboards' but Tailwind CSS is inherently mobile-first — unprefixed utilities apply to all sizes, and `sm:`/`md:`/`lg:` apply at minimum widths. Writing desktop-first in Tailwind requires using `max-*` variants which are less ergonomic and non-standard. This advice will confuse developers who then try to write desktop-first Tailwind and find themselves fighting the framework.
objections.suggested_fix: Clarify the nuance: 'Tailwind is mobile-first by default. For admin dashboards, DESIGN desktop-first (start your design thinking at desktop) but IMPLEMENT mobile-first in Tailwind (write base styles for mobile, then add `md:`/`lg:` overrides for desktop). Alternatively, use `max-md:` variants for specific mobile overrides if the desktop layout is your natural base.' This distinction between design thinking and implementation approach matters.

suggestions.description: The pattern reference table and decision trees cover happy-path UI but completely omit error states, empty states, loading states, and 404/error pages. These are among the most commonly neglected UI patterns and exactly where developers need gallery-quality inspiration. saaspo.com specifically curates empty state patterns. Add a section covering: empty states (first-use, no-results, error), loading skeletons, error boundaries with recovery actions, and toast/notification patterns.

Missing several high-impact anti-patterns: (1) Using `onClick` on `<div>` elements instead of `<button>` (partially covered but should be more explicit about event handlers), (2) Fixed-height containers with overflow:hidden that clip content at different font sizes, (3) Using `gap` without fallback on older Safari (increasingly less relevant but still in enterprise), (4) Nesting interactive elements (button inside a link), (5) Color as the ONLY indicator of state (fails for colorblind users). The existing anti-patterns are good but skew toward visual/performance concerns and underweight interaction/semantic issues.

Only two code exemplars are provided (hero and card). For a skill claiming to cover navigation, CTAs, pricing, forms, and animation, this is thin. Add at minimum: a sticky nav with mobile drawer, a CTA contrast band, and a scroll-reveal wrapper component. These are the patterns developers struggle with most and where concrete code has the highest ROI. They don't need to be exhaustive — 20-line focused examples are sufficient.

Principle 7 mentions Core Web Vitals targets (LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms) but nowhere in the skill is there guidance on how the recommended patterns affect these metrics. For example: hero images need `fetchpriority="high"` and `loading="eager"`, below-fold images need `loading="lazy"`, font loading strategy affects LCP. Add a brief performance checklist tied to the patterns you recommend — e.g., 'Hero images: use `` with fetchpriority="high", explicit dimensions, and preload the LCP image in ``.'

The scope check says 'For single-component requests, skip directly to Step 3' but Step 3 is the MCP tool call. It should say 'skip directly to Step 7 with a condensed Layout + Pattern + Code output' or reorganize so the code delivery step is correctly referenced. This is a process flow bug.

While the content is high quality, Claude Code command files that are too large can crowd out context window space for actual code. Consider: (1) Moving the full pattern decision trees into a separate markdown file that gets `@`-referenced, (2) Keeping the command file focused on the process steps and MCP integration, (3) Using the separate reference file for the detailed pattern catalogs and anti-patterns. objections.description: Anti-patterns are listed as statements but lack visual/verbal remedies. For example, 'Hero carousels' is flagged but the skill doesn't explain what to use instead for multi-image hero needs (video backgrounds, static feature strips, scrolling logos).
objections.suggested_fix: For each anti-pattern, add a 1-sentence remedy. E.g., 'Hero carousels → Use a single impactful static image or video background instead.'

objections.description: The skill repeatedly references specific gallery URLs (curated.design, landing.love, etc.) but these are external links that may rot, change, or be unavailable. The skill should not depend on external URLs for its core value.
objections.suggested_fix: Remove live URLs from the skill. Keep only the gallery names as pattern category labels. The value should be in the skill's codified patterns, not in linking to external sites that may disappear.

suggestions.description: The 8-step process (Understand → Gather → Consult MCP → Analyze → Plan → Generate Prompt → Code → Deliver) is too linear. Consider making it context-sensitive: if user provides code input, skip directly to pattern improvement. If user asks a single question, skip to deliver. Add a 'quick mode' for simple queries.

The prompt for vague input ('make it look better') asks the user to pick from options (a) through (j). This is good but the response format should be clearly defined so the skill can parse it reliably.

The animation section mentions 'View Transitions API' but doesn't mention the browser compatibility caveats or fallback strategies for Safari/Firefox. This could lead developers to ship broken animations.

The skill recommends '@container queries (Tailwind @container variant)' but doesn't mention that container queries require the container-type CSS property on the parent. This will confuse developers who just add the class.

The skill includes accessibility guidance but misses important modern patterns: autofocus management, aria-describedby for form errors, role='alert' for toast notifications, and the difference between aria-modal and role='dialog'.

The persona is 'senior UI/UX engineer' but the skill reads more like a textbook. Consider adding more conversational framing for recommendations, e.g., 'I'd recommend X because Y' instead of just 'Decision tree: X → Y'.

objections.description: File reading instructions are vague and don't match Claude Code's actual capabilities. Says 'Check src/components/' and 'Read tailwind.config.*' without specifying how the agent should actually read files (no mention of read_file tool or glob patterns). This will cause the agent to hallucinate file contents.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with explicit Claude Code file operations: 'Use read_file to examine package.json, tailwind.config.js/ts, and glob src/components/**/*.{tsx,ts} to identify existing patterns. Handle missing files gracefully with fallback defaults.'

objections.description: MCP tool calls reference undefined tool signatures (mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources, etc.). No error handling for tool failures, no schema validation for tool responses, and no fallback behavior if tools return malformed data. This will break silently.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit tool schemas, wrap calls in try-catch equivalents, and define concrete fallback behavior: 'If tool returns error or empty response, proceed with built-in patterns and notify user that MCP enhancement was unavailable.'

objections.description: Contradiction between accessibility requirements and code examples. Baseline specifies 'focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2' but Hero and Card examples use 'focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2'. This inconsistency will confuse developers about which pattern to follow.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize on one approach. Either update baseline to match the code examples (outline-based) or update all code examples to use ring-based focus indicators. Recommend ring-based for better visibility across backgrounds.

objections.description: CSS-only column lists 'animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4' which are NOT standard Tailwind classes — these require the tailwindcss-animate plugin. This misleads developers into thinking these are available by default, causing broken builds.
objections.suggested_fix: Either mark these as plugin-required ('tailwindcss-animate plugin required'), or replace with actual standard Tailwind utilities like 'animate-fade-in' with custom keyframes shown in config example.

objections.description: Code examples use semantic color tokens (text-primary, bg-card, text-muted-foreground) that require tailwind.config customization, but no config snippet is provided. Developers copying this code will get 'class not found' errors if they haven't set up these tokens.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a minimal tailwind.config.js snippet showing required color token definitions, or provide fallback examples using standard Tailwind colors (slate-900, slate-50, etc.) with a note to migrate to semantic tokens.

objections.description: States 'Define these in Tailwind config as zIndex theme extension' but provides no actual config snippet. This is actionable advice without the implementation details needed to execute it.
objections.suggested_fix: Add concrete tailwind.config.js example: 'theme: { extend: { zIndex: { sticky: 40, dropdown: 50, modal: 60, toast: 70, tooltip: 80 } } }'

objections.description: References specific galleries (curated.design, landing.love, etc.) then immediately says 'Do not directly copy proprietary assets.' This creates legal ambiguity — are these galleries approved sources or just inspiration references?
objections.suggested_fix: Clarify: 'These galleries are referenced for pattern categorization only. All recommendations synthesize common design principles, not specific implementations from these sources.'

suggestions.description: Add explicit argument parsing for Claude Code's $ARGUMENTS format. Show how to handle multi-line input, file paths with spaces, and JSON-structured input if user provides it.

Define concrete complexity thresholds with measurable criteria: 'Invoke if: component count > 3, requires shared state, spans multiple routes, or needs auth integration.' Remove vague '>3 sections' language.
Add anti-pattern for 'using arbitrary Tailwind values (w-[347px]) instead of theme tokens' since this breaks design system consistency which the skill emphasizes.
Add verification step: 'After implementation, run: npx tailwindcss -i ./src/index.css -o ./dist/output.css to verify no unused class warnings and confirm design tokens are properly purged.'

objections.location: Step 7 — Code Patterns (Reference code exemplar — Hero)
objections.description: The hero code example claims to be 'raw Tailwind + React that works with zero additional dependencies' but uses shadcn/ui semantic color tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, bg-card, text-card-foreground, ring-border) that do NOT exist in vanilla Tailwind. objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Use actual Tailwind color utilities (bg-white, text-slate-900, text-slate-500) for zero-dependency examples, OR (2) Explicitly label examples as 'shadcn/ui semantic tokens' and provide the tailwind.config.js setup required to define these colors. objections.description: The CSS-only column shows `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4` as a Tailwind class, but these are from the tailwindcss-animate plugin, NOT vanilla Tailwind. This directly contradicts the 'CSS-only (Tailwind)' label and the claim of zero dependencies.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with actual vanilla CSS/Tailwind: `animate-[fadeSlideUp_0.3s_ease-out]` with a custom @keyframes definition, OR clearly note that tailwindcss-animate plugin is required and remove it from the 'CSS-only' column. Add a true CSS-only row using opacity/transform with utility classes.

objections.description: Step 1 says to ask the user for clarification if input is vague, but Step 2 immediately proceeds to gather repo context without a clear pause point. The agent will barrel forward without waiting for user response, breaking the intended flow.

objections.description: The MCP tool calls are defined with parameter names but no guidance on handling failures. What if recommend_ui_sources returns empty? What if plan_ui_execution errors? The skill has no fallback logic.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit error handling: 'If MCP tool call fails or returns empty results, log the issue and fall back to built-in pattern knowledge. Do not halt the process—proceed with Step 4 using internal patterns.'

objections.description: Several essential patterns have decision trees but NO implementation code: Navigation (sticky, transparent-on-scroll, mobile drawer), CTAs, Forms, Pricing, Empty states, Loading states, Error states, Toasts/notifications. The skill describes what to use but not how to build it.
objections.suggested_fix: Add at minimum: (1) Sticky nav with transparent-on-scroll code, (2) Toast notification with z-index, (3) Loading skeleton, (4) Form with inline validation. These are high-frequency needs that shouldn't require external lookup.

objections.location: Step 4 — Color & Dark Mode section vs Step 7 code examples
objections.description: Step 4 defines semantic colors as `--color-surface`, `--color-text-primary`, `--color-text-muted`, `--color-border`, but Step 7 code uses `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-card`. The naming convention is inconsistent within the skill itself.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize on ONE naming convention throughout. Either use Tailwind CSS v4 syntax (--color-*) or shadcn/ui convention (background, foreground), but not both. Update all references to match.

objections.description: The accessibility principles are comprehensive but missing concrete implementation code. Developers need to see: skip-to-content link implementation, focus-visible ring in action, and aria-live region setup.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a compact 'Accessibility Quick-Start' code block with: (1) Skip link component, (2) Focus-visible button example, (3) Toast with aria-live. These are copy-paste ready and reinforce the non-negotiable stance.

--- description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions for Vite + React + Tailwind CSS projects. --- You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns commonly featured in top design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. When recommending patterns, ground them in established design principles (Gestalt grouping, Fitts's law, F-pattern scanning, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy) rather than claiming specific gallery sourcing. ## MCP Tool Policy Before calling any MCP tool (`mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources`, `mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution`, `mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt`), check if it is available in the current session. If a tool call fails, returns empty results, or the tool is not registered, **silently fall back to built-in recommendations**. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user. Never halt the process due to MCP unavailability. The parameter schemas shown in this skill are illustrative — adapt to the actual tool schema if it differs. ## Quick Pattern Reference | Task | Pattern Category | Top Patterns | |------|-----------------|-------------| | Hero | Landing pages | split, centered, full-bleed, gradient-mesh | | Nav | Navigation | sticky, transparent-on-scroll, pill, mega-menu | | CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form | | Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal | | Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed | | Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction | | Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier | | Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard | | States | Feedback | empty state, error boundary, loading skeleton, toast | | Footer | Site-wide | multi-column links, minimal app bar, newsletter | | Auth | Identity | login card, social OAuth row, magic-link input | | Testimonials | Social proof | quote card, avatar strip, logo cloud | ## Design Pattern Sources These gallery names represent **pattern categories**. All recommendations synthesize common design principles these galleries exemplify — not specific copyrighted implementations. Do not directly copy proprietary assets. | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns | |---------|--------|-------------| | **curated.design** | Web Design | Clean layouts, generous whitespace, type hierarchy, grid systems, editorial aesthetics | | **landing.love** | Landing Pages | Hero sections, social proof strips, feature grids, pricing tables, testimonial carousels | | **saaspo.com** | SaaS Websites | Dashboard layouts, onboarding flows, pricing pages, feature showcases, empty states | | **navbar.gallery** | Navigation | Sticky headers, transparent-on-scroll, mega menus, mobile drawers, pill navbars | | **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars | | **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states | ## Accessibility Baseline (Non-Negotiable) All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA: - **Color contrast**: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components. Color must never be the **only** indicator of state — always pair with icon, text, or shape change. - **Focus indicators**: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` on all interactive elements (standardized — all code examples use ring-based focus) - **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals - **Semantic HTML**: ` ); } ``` **Reference — Hero split layout (vanilla Tailwind):** ```tsx export function HeroSplit() { return (

Otherwise, provide this sequence: 1. **Tokens** — add/update design tokens (colors, spacing, z-index) in Tailwind config or CSS `@theme` 2. **Atoms** — build/update atomic components (buttons, inputs, badges) 3. **Sections** — compose section components (hero, nav, feature grid, CTA) 4. **Layout** — assemble page layout with sections 5. **Motion** — add animation and interaction (CSS-first, then framer-motion if needed) 6. **Responsive** — test at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px breakpoints; verify dark mode 7. **Accessibility audit** — keyboard navigation, screen reader, contrast check 8. **Performance** — verify LCP < 2.5s (hero image priority), CLS < 0.1 (explicit dimensions), INP < 200ms **For complex tasks** (>3 components, cross-page flows, shared state, auth-gated features): if MCP `generate_ui_agent_prompt` is available, use it. Otherwise, produce a structured implementation prompt covering: component tree, props interfaces, state management approach, and file locations. ### Step 7 — Deliver **For single components**, use condensed format: ``` ## [Component Name] **Pattern**: [chosen pattern + why] **Code**: [implementation using detected color/animation strategy] **Notes**: [responsive, dark mode, accessibility considerations] ``` **For pages/multi-component tasks**, use full format: ``` ## Design Recommendation: [Component/Page Name] ### Rationale [Design principles this draws from and why these patterns fit the context] ### Layout [Grid, spacing, hierarchy decisions] ### Components [Specific patterns for each UI element with decision rationale] ### Motion [Animation recommendations — CSS-only preferred, framer-motion when justified] ### Responsive [Breakpoint strategy, container queries, image handling] ### Dark Mode [Color token strategy, contrast verification notes] ### Accessibility [Focus management, ARIA, keyboard, screen reader notes] ### Implementation [Code using detected libraries, or vanilla Tailwind + React] ### Performance Checklist - [ ] Hero/LCP image: fetchpriority="high", explicit dimensions, preload in - [ ] Below-fold images: loading="lazy" - [ ] Fonts: font-display: swap, preload critical font files - [ ] All images have width/height or aspect-ratio (CLS prevention) ### Optional Accelerators [Libraries that would speed up implementation if user wants them] ``` ## Z-Index Scale Establish this scale and never deviate. Values above `z-50` require Tailwind config extension: | Layer | Value | Tailwind class | |-------|-------|---------------| | Base content | 0 | `z-0` | | Sticky headers/nav | 40 | `z-40` | | Dropdowns/popovers | 50 | `z-50` | | Modals/dialogs | 60 | `z-[60]` | | Toast notifications | 70 | `z-[70]` | | Tooltips | 80 | `z-[80]` | To use named classes instead of arbitrary values, extend Tailwind config: Tailwind v3: ```js // tailwind.config.js module.exports = { theme: { extend: { zIndex: { 60: '60', 70: '70', 80: '80' } } } } ``` Tailwind v4: ```css @theme { --z-index-60: 60; --z-index-70: 70; --z-index-80: 80; } ``` ## Design Principles (Always Apply) 1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye. Minimum 2:1 size ratio between heading levels. 2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality. When in doubt, add more space. 3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do. Everything else is secondary. 4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show spatial relationships. Never for decoration. 5. **Mobile-first implementation** — Tailwind is mobile-first. Write base styles for mobile, add `sm:`/`md:`/`lg:` for larger screens. For admin dashboards, *design* desktop-first but *implement* mobile-first. 6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product. Establish a system, then follow it. 7. **Performance is UX** — Target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms.
